Barack Obama (@BarackObama)

This interview, which is also available to listen to as a podcast, provides a useful overview of some of the dangerous trends we’ve been seeing in recent months regarding federalization and militarization of state and local police functions. nytimes.com/2025/08/27/opini…




— https://nitter.net/BarackObama/status/1961074450029318513#m

If any of you 200K are looking for a place to spend your journalism-supporting budget, I know of a pretty swell, hard-hitting newsletter. 

npr.org/2024/10/28/nx-s1-516…

New from me: Less than 5 percent of DA's offices have a unit tasked with seeking out wrongful convictions. I spoke with Marissa Bluestine of the Quattrone Center about what these units do well, what they could do better, and the obstacles they face.

(Link follows)

Despite (or because of) their success, CIUs now get pushback from Republican attorneys general, police departments who don't like to see their incompetence/corruption exposed, and city officials who fear the lawsuits wrought by exonerations.

Link:

radleybalko.substack.com/p/t…

. . . building his confidence and coaxing out the magnificent beast within him. I first met Wally five years into his life, and only got to see the marvelous result -- this loyal, determined, gentle, awkward, wonderfully empathetic fellow. Truly one of the Seven Great Dogs of his time. We will miss him terribly.

We say goodbye to this handsome gentleman today. 

My wife @LilianaSegura adopted Wally 15 years ago. I don't know that any dog and human could be more perfectly suited for one another. Wally was nearly feral when Liliana got him. She spent the next several years . . .

This is a good time to remember that immigration judges aren’t real judges. 

They work for DOJ as part of the executive branch (under Bondi and Trump) and the fact that we call them judges like they’re independent arbiters would be laughable in a free and fair country.
ICE agents detained a Texas father trying to deliver milk to his infant daughter in the neonatal intensive care unit, disregarding his status as a DACA recipient. trib.al/BOeMew3

It's been well reported that Kent is a virulent bigot who associates with white nationalists. 

But sure. He's "a good guy." A "very fine" guy, you might say.

This @the-independent.com story is such an obvious lift of mine that they didn’t even bother deleting my site’s referral URL. 

And no, they do not credit me for reporting it first or (intentionally) link to my story. 

In j-school, rewording someone else’s reporting was called plagiarism. 1/
Jason Isbell Explains How Music Is Like The News But For Sounds. Subscribe to The Onion at https://membership.theonion.com/?campaign=701a500001bfegPAAQ.

This would be the funniest outcome.

He gets in the SCIF and reveals that his colleagues sent him to a dangerous war zone on a top secret mission to hunt and kill snipes.

Hey, I know you're suffering because we waged a pointless, massively expensive, forever war that I promised would never happen if you elected us, and we did it without the slightest bit of preparation, which is compounding the pain, but hey, at least a bunch of foreigners are suffering more!

Um, what? 

Mullin is claiming that as a congressman, he was selected for some sort of mission that required training that was "hell," and was then sent somewhere where he "smelled war." But it's all classified. And only four people know about it. And he can't share any more details.
ICE commemorates St Patrick’s Day with a tear gas tweet

It is very crazy a judge ordered the DOGE depositions removed from YouTube. 

Watch more, subscribe here: www.youtube.com/watch?v=xtMn...

On the one hand, good. 

On the other, the vote that spurred this slapfight was a xenophobic Paul amendment to cut off funding for basic services to legally admitted refugees and people seeking asylum.

After Venezuela beat the fucking US in baseball for the tiniest shred of poetic justice, the President posted that it should become a US state.

This is quite literally the most unhinged and depraved instance of sore-loserhood in the history of sports. 

www.independent.co.uk/news/world/a...

AIPAC made a last-minute ad buy to promote Bushra Amiwala in #IL09, to split the progressive vote; then after the election took credit for helping defeat dangerous "would-be squad members" like Bushra Amiwala.

New at The Watch: 

The confirmation hearings for DHS nominee Markwayne Mullin begin today. Here are the immigration questions I think he should be asked.

radleybalko.substack.com/p/immigratio...

Bellingcat and our partners at @evidentmedia.org and @calmatters.org have tracked US Border Patrol agents, analysing over 85 hours of footage taken across the country to try and unpack the circumstances around multiple use-of-force incidents. www.bellingcat.com/news/2026/03...
thinking about the hero of this game for Team Venezuela, Eugenio Suárez, and his comments about ICE’s effect on Latino baseball players just last week
Just a reminder that Trump has said he can "take" Cuba or do "whatever he wants with it" and that this is utterly insane.

Yep. It's already extremely difficult. There's a provision that if the states meet certain requirements (better defense funding, etc.), they'd get even less federal review. Trump's DOJ is giving them less review without the requirements.

"This Constitution, and the Laws of the United States which shall be made in Pursuance thereof; and all Treaties made, or which shall be made, under the Authority of the United States, shall be the supreme Law of the Land"

Wow this investigation identifies 5 federal agents who "engaged in a pattern of force and questionable detention, aggressive tactics that courts have said likely violated the constitution, as they moved from Bakersfield to Los Angeles, and then Chicago and Minneapolis" calmatters.org/investigatio...
